payload.source_text: The pocket money tracker system allows children to claim pocket money tasks as they are completed throughout the week.
Parents can detail tasks that should be completed and the monetary amount earned for that task.
As a task is completed it is linked to the child that completed it.
The children should be able to see the available tasks and the tasks they have completed that week.
At the end of each week the children can see the total amount they have earned that week.
The Parents can also see a report of how much each child has earned and the tasks they completed so that they can provide the pocket money earned to each child.
At the start of the next week all tasks become available again to be claimed.
Historical task data shall be available.
The resulting system shall be able to operate on iOS 16, Android 9 and Windows 11 or later.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Source elements present in the ledger.

original_descriptions: Weekly pocket money task completion and tracking cycle
Enable children to claim and track completed pocket money tasks
Provide children visibility into task inventory availability and completion status

payload.name: Household Participant Roles
payload.description: Enterprise accountability for defining and recognizing the distinct participant roles within the household ecosystem, establishing who has standing to interact with the enterprise and in what capacity.

payload.name: Compensated Work Stewardship
payload.description: Enterprise commitment to governing the lifecycle of compensated work arrangements, including the definition of work opportunities, their monetary valuation, and the accountability record of work performed and claimed.

payload.name: Financial Obligation Transparency
payload.description: Enterprise responsibility for providing clear, timely visibility into earned compensation and ensuring stakeholders can understand and verify financial obligations owed within the defined compensation cycle.

payload.name: Performance Accountability
payload.description: Enterprise obligation to maintain authoritative records of participant performance, enabling oversight, verification of compensation distribution accuracy, and longitudinal analysis of contribution patterns.

payload.name: Task Specification Stewardship
payload.description: Business responsibility for establishing and governing the tasks offered within the household, including the monetary values attached to each task and their recurring availability within defined cycles.

payload.name: Completion Accountability
payload.description: Business responsibility for children to claim accountability for completed work, establishing the relationship between child effort and entitlement to reward recognition.

payload.name: Earnings Settlement Record
payload.description: Business responsibility for accumulating and presenting the monetary entitlement earned by children within defined periods, supporting the fulfillment of payment obligations by parents.

payload.name: Historical Activity Stewardship
payload.description: Business responsibility for preserving task and earnings records across time periods to enable auditability, trend analysis, and sustained accountability beyond current operational cycles.

payload.name: Task Lifecycle Model
payload.description: Logical representation of task state transitions, availability cycles, and historical persistence requirements governing how tasks move through their operational lifecycle from creation through completion and archival.

payload.name: Child Data Visibility Model
payload.description: Logical design of access boundaries and visibility constraints determining which task and earnings information child actors may view or interact with based on relational context and role-based scoping rules.

payload.name: Platform Version Compatibility
payload.description: Physical constraints governing operating system version requirements and compatibility boundaries for deployment infrastructure and runtime environments.

payload.name: Multi-Platform Deployment Configuration
payload.description: Detailed specifications for deploying application components across iOS, Android, and Windows desktop platforms, including platform-specific runtime configurations, build parameters, and deployment topology details.

payload.name: Child User Interface Specification
payload.description: Detailed interface specifications for child actor interactions, including precise UI component definitions for task claiming, viewing available tasks, completed task displays, and weekly earnings access timing parameters.

payload.name: Parent User Interface Specification
payload.description: Detailed interface specifications for parent actor interactions, including precise UI component definitions for task configuration controls and distribution report access screens.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Domain elements present in the ledger.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall recognise child participants as a distinct role within the household ecosystem.
payload.rationale: Establishes the enterprise's commitment to acknowledge child participants as a structurally distinct actor type with standing to interact with the enterprise, forming part of the household participant composition.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall recognise parent participants as a distinct role within the household ecosystem.
payload.rationale: Establishes the enterprise's commitment to acknowledge parent participants as a structurally distinct actor type with standing to interact with the enterprise, forming part of the household participant composition.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable authorised members to define compensated work assignments with associated monetary values.
payload.rationale: Combines the enterprise goal to enable parents (authorised members) to define task assignments with the functional capability of assigning compensation values. Both CCIs express a single composite obligation: the enterprise must provide the capability to create work opportunities with monetary compensation.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable authorised members to view the availability and completion status of compensated work opportunities.
payload.rationale: Expresses the enterprise commitment to provide visibility into work inventory, derived from the goal to enable children (authorised members) to view task inventory and the function of providing task visibility.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable authorised members to claim completed compensated work assignments.
payload.rationale: Derived from the goal to enable children to claim pocket money tasks and the tracking function. This is a distinct obligation from viewing: the enterprise must provide the capability to assert completion.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall maintain a record of completed compensated work assignments.
payload.rationale: The existence of the 'pocket money task completion record' entity and the tracking function imply a structural obligation: the enterprise must maintain this record as part of its accountability for compensated work stewardship.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall establish monetary compensation values for compensated work assignments.
payload.rationale: The 'monetary compensation value' entity represents a structural attribute that must exist within the enterprise's work stewardship model. This is the enterprise-level commitment to defining and maintaining compensation values.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall establish a weekly cycle for task completion, tracking, and earnings summary reporting.
payload.rationale: The When-column CCI establishes the enterprise's commitment to operate on a defined weekly cadence for compensation-related activities. This is a capability commitment at enterprise scope.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable children to view their weekly earnings summary.
payload.rationale: The Why-column Goal and How-column Function together express a single enterprise obligation to provide visibility. 'Enable' is appropriate Row 1 vocabulary for an enterprise capability commitment. Both CCIs contribute to this unified obligation.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall provide weekly earnings summaries.
payload.rationale: The How-column Function implies the enterprise's responsibility to produce and make available the earnings summary artifact. This is distinct from enabling viewing (previous requirement) — it addresses the enterprise's commitment to the summary itself.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall maintain authoritative records of participant performance.
payload.rationale: Establishes the enterprise's accountability for maintaining task history records as the authoritative source for performance data.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable access to child performance reporting for compensation distribution oversight.
payload.rationale: Consolidates the goal of enabling parents to access performance reports with the entity and function that deliver that capability.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall provide stakeholders access to longitudinal task history for accountability and trend analysis.
payload.rationale: Expresses the enterprise commitment to enable longitudinal access for oversight and pattern analysis over time.

payload.statement: The parent user shall be accountable for establishing tasks within the household.
payload.rationale: HOW-column business capability declaration identifying the parent user as the accountable role for task specification; surfaces the business responsibility for task establishment.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain a record of each task offered within the household.
payload.rationale: WHAT-column entity representing a business artefact that must be maintained at the business-owner level; tasks are the core offering subject to stewardship.

payload.statement: The business shall record a monetary value for each task.
payload.rationale: WHAT-column attribute combined with HOW-column capability declaration; the business must be able to associate and record monetary values as part of task specification stewardship.

payload.statement: The business shall govern task availability according to a weekly cycle.
payload.rationale: HOW-column business rule combined with WHEN-column temporal cycle; the weekly reset is a normative business governance constraint on task availability that the business must enforce.
payload.fit_criteria: Task availability state resets at the conclusion of each seven-day cycle.

payload.statement: The child user shall be able to claim accountability for completed work.
payload.rationale: Business capability for the child role to assert completion accountability, derived from the Actor (Child user), Process (claim completed task), and Relationship (child claims completed task) CCIs at the business responsibility level.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain a record of task completion status for each child's work.
payload.rationale: Business responsibility to record the completion state of tasks, triggered by task completion events, supporting accountability and downstream reward tracking.

payload.statement: The business shall make available child completion claims for monetary reward tracking.
payload.rationale: Business capability to support the goal of enabling monetary reward tracking by making completion claims accessible for downstream processes.

payload.statement: The business shall enforce child visibility limited to available and completed tasks within temporal scope.
payload.rationale: Business governance rule constraining what task information is made visible to child users, derived from the Why-column constraint CCI.

payload.statement: A child completion claim shall associate a child with a completed task.
payload.rationale: Structural business requirement defining the relationship between Child entity and completed tasks, surfaced from the Entity and Relationship CCIs through object recursion.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain a record of the weekly earnings total for each child.
payload.rationale: Business responsibility to maintain the calculated weekly earnings total as a business artefact, derived from WHAT-column entity, HOW-column aggregation function, and WHEN-column weekly cycle.

payload.statement: The business shall record the monetary value earned by each child from each task.
payload.rationale: Business capability to record the child-task-earnings relationship as reported by parents, derived from WHAT-column relationship and HOW-column reporting function.

payload.statement: The Parent user shall be responsible for reporting child-task-earnings relationships.
payload.rationale: Business role accountability for providing earnings data, derived from WHO-column actor and HOW-column reporting function.

payload.statement: The business shall aggregate child earnings on a weekly cycle.
payload.rationale: Business capability to perform periodic aggregation aligned with the weekly business cycle, derived from WHEN-column cycle and HOW-column aggregation function.

payload.statement: The business shall make available earnings records to support parent payment fulfillment.
payload.rationale: Business responsibility to make earnings totals accessible for payment purposes, derived from WHY-column goal and WHAT-column earnings entity.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain a record of historical task data.
payload.rationale: The WHAT-column entity (historical task data) and HOW-column rule (must be retained) together establish the business responsibility to maintain records of task activity over time.

payload.statement: The business shall make historical task data accessible across time periods.
payload.rationale: The WHY-column constraint (must remain accessible across periods) expresses a governance rule on the availability of historical records, distinct from the capability to maintain them.

payload.statement: The system shall maintain a logical association between each task completion event and the child entity that triggered it.
payload.rationale: Derives from the What-column relationship CCI describing how task completion events are structurally linked to child entities; this logical association supports traceability and state transition logic.

payload.statement: The system shall transition tasks to an available state on a weekly cycle.
payload.rationale: Captures the When-column cycle CCI governing temporal state transitions; expresses the logical behavior by which task availability is restored periodically without specifying implementation technology.
payload.fit_criteria: All tasks eligible for availability restoration transition state within one cycle period.

payload.statement: The system shall enforce that historical task records remain logically persisted and accessible.
payload.rationale: Why-column constraint requiring that completed or archived task data must not be lost or made inaccessible; expresses a logical integrity rule governing lifecycle persistence.
payload.fit_criteria: All historical task records created since system inception remain queryable.

payload.statement: The system shall enforce data visibility constraints that limit child user access to task collections based on relational context and role-based scoping rules.
payload.rationale: This is a logical access boundary constraint derived from a How-column Rule CCI; it restricts which task collections child actors may view or interact with, enforcing a design-level invariant without specifying implementation technology.
payload.fit_criteria: A child user shall not be able to access task collections outside the scope permitted by their relational context and assigned role.

payload.statement: The system shall derive a weekly earned amount for each child based on completed task data.
payload.rationale: This is a logical derivation capability sourced from a How-column Function CCI; the system computes an earnings aggregate for child presentation without specifying algorithmic detail or output format.

payload.statement: The system shall derive aggregated child earnings and task completion data for parent visibility.
payload.rationale: This is a logical reporting capability sourced from a How-column Function CCI; the system aggregates child-level metrics for parent actors without specifying report format or delivery mechanism.

payload.statement: The system shall support deployment on operating system versions within the compatibility boundaries specified for each target platform.
payload.rationale: Physical constraint derived from Why-column CCI governing OS version compatibility limits that restrict deployment target selection and runtime platform choices.

payload.statement: The system shall deploy the mobile application components to iOS runtime environments with minimum iOS version 14.0 and support for Swift 5.5 runtime.
payload.rationale: Row 5 detailed specification of the iOS deployment node, specifying the precise platform version and runtime requirements that an implementer must configure before deployment.
payload.fit_criteria: Deployment artifacts successfully install and execute on iOS 14.0+ devices with Swift 5.5 runtime present.

payload.statement: The system shall deploy the mobile application components to Android runtime environments with minimum API level 26 (Android 8.0) and support for Kotlin 1.7 runtime.
payload.rationale: Row 5 detailed specification of the Android deployment node, specifying the precise API level and runtime requirements that an implementer must configure before deployment.
payload.fit_criteria: Deployment artifacts successfully install and execute on Android devices running API level 26 or higher with Kotlin 1.7 runtime present.

payload.statement: The system shall deploy the desktop application components to Windows runtime environments with minimum Windows 10 version 1809 and .NET 6.0 runtime installed.
payload.rationale: Row 5 detailed specification of the Windows desktop deployment node, specifying the precise operating system version and runtime framework requirements that an implementer must configure before deployment.
payload.fit_criteria: Deployment artifacts successfully install and execute on Windows 10 version 1809+ systems with .NET 6.0 runtime present.

payload.statement: The child user interface shall provide a task claiming operation component.
payload.rationale: Specifies the implementation requirement for the child actor's task claiming interface component at the detailed design level.

payload.statement: The child user interface shall provide a component for viewing available tasks.
payload.rationale: Defines the detailed interface requirement for displaying the available task list to child users.

payload.statement: The child user interface shall provide a component for viewing completed tasks.
payload.rationale: Defines the detailed interface requirement for displaying the completed task list to child users.

payload.statement: The system shall make weekly earnings totals accessible to child actors on a weekly cycle.
payload.rationale: Specifies the timing cycle for child actor access to earnings data, defining when the system shall grant access.
payload.fit_criteria: Earnings totals are accessible at the beginning of each weekly cycle period.

payload.statement: The system shall reset task availability for child actors on a weekly cycle.
payload.rationale: Defines the weekly reset cycle timing for task availability, specifying when the system shall refresh the available task list.
payload.fit_criteria: Task availability is reset at the beginning of each weekly cycle period.

payload.statement: The Parent user interface component shall provide controls for task configuration operations, including assignment rule specification, target child selection, and task parameter definition.
payload.rationale: Specifies the detailed functional obligations of the parent interface component for task configuration, identifying the precise control categories required for implementation.

payload.statement: The Parent user interface component shall include a distribution report access screen that displays completed task outcomes, reward allocations, and child performance metrics.
payload.rationale: Defines the detailed interface requirement for report access functionality, specifying the data elements that must be presented to the parent actor.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Requirement elements present in the ledger.

payload.title: High Level Description
payload.description: Section: High Level Description

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Segment elements present in the ledger.

payload.description: Expresses the fundamental purpose of enabling children to claim and track completed pocket money tasks weekly.
Articulates parental motivation to define compensated task assignments with associated monetary values.
Captures the purpose of providing children visibility into available and completed task inventory.
Identifies the motivational goal of enabling children to view their weekly earnings summary.
Establishes parental need to access child performance reporting for accurate compensation distribution purposes.
Indicates stakeholder requirement for longitudinal task history access supporting accountability or trend analysis.
Section header indicating the commencement of conceptual system description.
Establishes the core business concept of children claiming completed tasks for monetary reward tracking.
Defines the parent capability to specify task entities with associated monetary value attributes.
Specifies the relationship between completed task entities and child entities through linkage.
Defines child actor visibility requirements for both available and completed task entities within temporal scope.
Establishes weekly aggregation capability for earned monetary totals visible to child actors.
Defines parent reporting capability displaying child-task-earnings relationships to support payment fulfillment.
Specifies the cyclical business rule for task availability reset at weekly temporal boundaries.
Mandates retention and accessibility of historical task data entities across temporal periods.
Logical association rule defining how task completion events are linked to child entities.
Data visibility requirements specifying logical access patterns for child users to task collections.
Temporal aggregation logic defining weekly computation and presentation of earned amounts to children.
Logical reporting interface providing parents aggregated view of child earnings and task completion data.
Periodic state reset logic defining weekly cycle behavior for task availability restoration.
Data retention requirement mandating logical persistence and accessibility of historical task records.
Platform compatibility constraint specifying concrete operating system versions for deployment.
Identifies children as operational actors who execute task claiming activities throughout the week on a temporal basis.
Identifies parents as operational actors responsible for task configuration and monetary value assignment activities.
Defines operational visibility requirements for child actors viewing available and completed tasks within weekly cycles.
Establishes weekly operational schedule boundary when child actors can access earned amount totals.
Defines parent operational role in accessing reports to execute pocket money distribution activities.
Specifies weekly operational cycle timing when task availability resets for claiming operations.
Defines deployment platform locations specifying where the system operates across iOS, Android, and Windows environments.
